# scalatags-datastar

> Scalatags bindings for the [Datastar](https://data-star.dev) hypermedia framework that go beyond
> plain bindings to **fully statically-typed templates**: signal references, the expression language,
> and the backend endpoint URLs referenced by `@get`/`@post`/ŌĆ” are all checked by the Scala 3
> compiler. Backend endpoints are [Tapir](https://tapir.softwaremill.com) endpoints, so a template can
> only reference a route that actually exists, and its URL is reverse-routed from the endpoint ŌĆö they
> cannot drift.

The library types the three magic-string kinds Datastar drives reactivity from ŌĆö `data-*` attributes,
signal/expression strings, and action URLs ŌĆö and speaks Datastar's SSE wire protocol back from the
server with the *same* Scalatags fragments and the *same* signal case class. The binding layer
cross-compiles to JVM and Scala.js; the SSE codec and the server bridge are JVM-only. Datastar **Pro**
attributes are not yet bound.

groupId `works.iterative`, Scala 3.3.8 (LTS), built with Mill. Pre-release ŌĆö not yet on Maven Central;
build from source.

## Imports

A template author reaches the whole surface through three library wildcards plus the Scalatags peer:

- `import scalatags.Text.all.*` ŌĆö Scalatags tags/attributes (platform peer; always required).
- `import works.iterative.scalatags.datastar.Datastar.*` ŌĆö the core DSL: the `data-*` builders, the
  `Expr` expression DSL (`lit` and the operators), the `Signal`/`Signals` model, and `ActionOptions`.
  Do **not** also import `Expr.*` ŌĆö two equal wildcards make `lit` ambiguous; this one star is enough.
- `import works.iterative.scalatags.datastar.tapir.*` ŌĆö the `endpoint.action` extension that
  reverse-routes a Tapir endpoint to a Datastar action. Cross-compiled (JVM + JS).
- `import works.iterative.scalatags.datastar.tapir.sse.*` ŌĆö server side only: `SignalsInput`,
  `datastarEvents`, `datastarStream`, and a re-export of the SSE codec (`ServerSentEvents`,
  `ElementPatchMode`, `readSignals`).

## The four typed surfaces

- **Attributes** ŌĆö the full standard `data-*` surface with a typed, chainable modifier builder; each
  attribute exposes only the modifiers Datastar accepts, e.g.
  `input(dataOn("input").debounce(300.millis).once := "@get('/search')")`.
- **Signals + expressions** ŌĆö the signal store is a **case class**, the single source of truth for its
  shape and initial values:
  ```scala
  final case class Counter(count: Int = 0, step: Int = 1) derives Signals
  object Counter extends Signals.Handles[Counter]:
    val count = signal("count")   // Signal[Int]; signal("nope") would not compile
  ```
  `derives Signals` yields the initial `data-signals` JSON via `dataSignals := Counter()`; the
  companion handles give field-checked typed references. `Expr[A]` operators render with JavaScript
  precedence; equality is `===`/`!==` (Scala cannot override `==`); numeric operators are gated by
  `Numeric`. `dataBind := Counter.step` renders the bare signal name (two-way).
- **Backend actions** ŌĆö `endpoint.action(input)` derives the verb and reverse-routes the URL from a
  Tapir endpoint, so a template can only reference a route that exists:
  `button(dataOn("click") := toggle.action(7L))` ŌåÆ `@post('/todos/7/toggle')`. Total ŌĆö a methodless or
  non-action endpoint falls back to `@get`, mirroring Tapir's own client interpreter. Action options
  are typed via `ActionOptions` (`contentType`, `headers`); only the fields you set render.
- **Server-side SSE** ŌĆö `ServerSentEvents.patchElements(frag, selector, mode, useViewTransition,
  namespace, ŌĆ”)` / `patchSignals(model)` render the exact Datastar wire format (validated against the
  official SDK conformance suite); `readSignals[A](json)` decodes the round-tripped store into the
  *same* case class that seeds it. On a Tapir/ZIO server, `SignalsInput.body[A]` / `.query[A]` decode
  the inbound store in the codec layer (a misfit payload is a `400` before the handler runs),
  `.out(datastarEvents)` declares the `text/event-stream`, and `datastarStream(events*)` (or its
  `ZStream[String]` overload, for over-time feeds) builds the response byte stream.

## Wire-format gotchas (verified against Datastar v1.0.2)

These bit the examples gallery and are not obvious from the typed API:

- **Two request channels, chosen by verb.** `@get`/`@delete` append the whole signal store as a
  `?datastar=<json>` query parameter (with no signals it is the empty object `?datastar=%7B%7D`);
  `@post`/`@put`/`@patch` send it as a JSON body. Decode with `SignalsInput.query[A]` for the former,
  `SignalsInput.body[A]` for the latter.
- **Checkbox group ŌåÆ array of booleans.** Every checkbox in the group shares the *bare* signal name
  (`data-bind="selections"`, no index); the client collects them into a boolean array by DOM order.
  `data-bind="selections[0]"` does **not** work ŌĆö the client treats the whole string as one flat key,
  so the array stays empty (request still 200, silently a no-op).
- **File input ŌåÆ array of objects.** `<input type=file multiple data-bind="upload">` encodes each file
  as `{name, contents, mime}`, where `contents` is raw base64 (no `data:ŌĆ”,` prefix). Model it as a
  case class, not a `List[String]` of base64 (that fails to decode ŌåÆ 400).
- **Pin the client to v1.0.2.** The `datastar-patch-elements`/`-signals` event names the codec targets
  landed in the GitHub stable line; npm "latest"/beta still ships the old `merge-*` names. CDN:
  `cdn.jsdelivr.net/gh/starfederation/datastar@v1.0.2/bundles/datastar.js`.

## Modules

- `datastar` (JVM + JS) ŌĆö the core DSL: `data-*` attributes, the `Expr` DSL, the `Signals` model.
  Depends only on Scalatags.
- `tapir` (JVM + JS) ŌĆö the `endpoint.action` reverse-routing bridge; compile-depends on core.
- `sse` (JVM) ŌĆö the native SSE codec and `readSignals`; Scalatags + zio-json, stack-neutral.
- `tapirsse` (JVM) ŌĆö the Tapir + SSE server bridge: the signal-store input codecs and the event-stream
  output plus response-stream builder.
- `scenarios` (JVM) ŌĆö a runnable dogfood app reimplementing the
  [data-star.dev/examples](https://data-star.dev/examples) gallery with these bindings (every example
  but the Pro-only `match_media`), each demo shown beside the typed Scala that produces it.

## Documentation

- [README.md](README.md): full overview with worked examples for every layer and the runnable app.
- [PLAN.md](PLAN.md): design, architecture decisions, roadmap, and verified Datastar reference notes.
- Source: core DSL in `datastar/src`, the Tapir bridge in `tapir/src`, the SSE codec in `sse/src`, the
  server bridge in `tapirsse/src`, the worked examples in `scenarios/src`.
- GitHub: <https://github.com/iterative-works/scalatags-datastar>
